61_1_1.NKJV: Simon Peter, a bondservant and apostle of Jesus Christ, To those who have obtained like precious faith with us by the righteousness of our God and Savior Jesus Christ:

61_1_1.ESV: Simeon  Peter, a servant  and apostle of Jesus Christ, To those who have obtained a faith of equal standing with ours by the righteousness of our God and Savior Jesus Christ:
61_1_1.NASB: Simon Peter, a bond-servant and apostle of Jesus Christ, To those who have received a faith of the same kind as ours, by the righteousness of our God and Savior, Jesus Christ:
61_1_1.NLT: This letter is from Simon Peter, a slave and apostle of Jesus Christ. I am writing to you who share the same precious faith we have. This faith was given to you because of the justice and fairness of Jesus Christ, our God and Savior.
61_1_1.WEB: Simon Peter, a servant and apostle of Jesus Christ, to those who have obtained a like precious faith with us in the righteousness of our God and Savior, Jesus Christ:
61_1_1.CSB17: Simeon <sup>[1]</sup> Peter, <sup>ⓗ</sup> a servant and an apostle <sup>ⓘ</sup> of Jesus Christ: To those who have received a faith equal to ours through the righteousness <sup>ⓙ</sup> of our God and Savior <sup>ⓚ</sup> Jesus Christ.
61_1_1.AMP:
Simon Peter, a bond-servant and apostle (special messenger, personally chosen representative) of Jesus Christ,
To those who have received and possess [by God's will] a precious faith of the same kind as ours, by the righteousness of our God and Savior, Jesus Christ:
61_1_1.MSG: I, Simon Peter, am a servant and apostle of Jesus Christ. I write this to you whose experience with God is as life-changing as ours, all due to our God's straight dealing and the intervention of our God and Savior, Jesus Christ.
61_1_1.NIV:
Simon Peter, a servant and apostle of Jesus Christ,
To those who through the righteousness of our God and Savior Jesus Christ have received a faith as precious as ours:
61_1_1.NRSVCE: Simeon Peter, a servant and apostle of Jesus Christ, To those who have received a faith as precious as ours through the righteousness of our God and Savior Jesus Christ:

61_1_2.NKJV: Grace and peace be multiplied to you in the knowledge of God and of Jesus our Lord,
61_1_2.KJV: Grace<S>5485</S> and<S>2532</S> peace<S>1515</S> be multiplied<S>4129</S> unto you<S>5213</S> through<S>1722</S> the knowledge<S>1922</S> of God<S>2316</S>, and<S>2532</S> of Jesus<S>2424</S> our<S>2257</S> Lord<S>2962</S>,
61_1_2.ESV: May grace and peace be multiplied to you in the knowledge of God and of Jesus our Lord.
61_1_2.NASB: Grace and peace be multiplied to you in the knowledge of God and of Jesus our Lord;
61_1_2.NLT: May God give you more and more grace and peace as you grow in your knowledge of God and Jesus our Lord.
61_1_2.WEB: Grace to you and peace be multiplied in the knowledge of God and of Jesus our Lord,
61_1_2.CSB17: May grace and peace be multiplied to you through the knowledge of God <sup>ⓛ</sup> and of Jesus our Lord.
61_1_2.AMP: Grace and peace [that special sense of spiritual well-being] be multiplied to you in the [true, intimate] knowledge of God and of Jesus our Lord.
61_1_2.MSG: Grace and peace to you many times over as you deepen in your experience with God and Jesus, our Master.
61_1_2.NIV: Grace and peace be yours in abundance through the knowledge of God and of Jesus our Lord.
61_1_2.NRSVCE: May grace and peace be yours in abundance in the knowledge of God and of Jesus our Lord.

61_1_3.NKJV: as His divine power has given to us all things that pertain to life and godliness, through the knowledge of Him who called us by glory and virtue,

61_1_3.ESV: His divine power has granted to us all things that pertain to life and godliness, through the knowledge of him who called us to  his own glory and excellence,
61_1_3.NASB: seeing that His divine power has granted to us everything pertaining to life and godliness, through the true knowledge of Him who called us by His own glory and excellence.
61_1_3.NLT: By his divine power, God has given us everything we need for living a godly life. We have received all of this by coming to know him, the one who called us to himself by means of his marvelous glory and excellence.
61_1_3.WEB: seeing that his divine power has granted to us all things that pertain to life and godliness, through the knowledge of him who called us by his own glory and virtue;
61_1_3.CSB17: His <sup>[2]</sup> divine power <sup>ⓜ</sup> has given us everything required for life and godliness through the knowledge <sup>ⓝ</sup> of him who called <sup>ⓐ</sup> us by <sup>[3]</sup> his own glory and goodness.
61_1_3.AMP: For His divine power has bestowed on us [absolutely] everything necessary for [a dynamic spiritual] life and godliness, through true and personal knowledge of Him who called us by His own glory and excellence.
61_1_3.MSG: Everything that goes into a life of pleasing God has been miraculously given to us by getting to know, personally and intimately, the One who invited us to God. The best invitation we ever received!
61_1_3.NIV:
Making One’s Calling and Election Sure
His divine power has given us everything we need for life and godliness through our knowledge of him who called us by his own glory and goodness.
61_1_3.NRSVCE: His divine power has given us everything needed for life and godliness, through the knowledge of him who called us by his own glory and goodness.

61_1_4.NKJV: by which have been given to us exceedingly great and precious promises, that through these you may be partakers of the divine nature, having escaped the corruption that is in the world through lust.
61_1_4.KJV: Whereby<S>1223</S> <S>3739</S> are given<S>1433</S> unto us<S>2254</S> exceeding great<S>3176</S> and<S>2532</S> precious<S>5093</S> promises<S>1862</S>: that<S>2443</S> by<S>1223</S> these<S>5130</S> ye might be<S>1096</S> partakers<S>2844</S> of the divine<S>2304</S> nature<S>5449</S>, having escaped<S>668</S> the corruption<S>5356</S> that is in<S>1722</S> the world<S>2889</S> through<S>1722</S> lust<S>1939</S>.
61_1_4.ESV: by which he has granted to us his precious and very great promises, so that through them you may become partakers of the divine nature, having escaped from the corruption that is in the world because of sinful desire.
61_1_4.NASB: For by these He has granted to us His precious and magnificent promises, so that by them you may become partakers of [the] divine nature, having escaped the corruption that is in the world by lust.

61_1_4.MSG: We were also given absolutely terrific promises to pass on to you—your tickets to participation in the life of God after you turned your back on a world corrupted by lust.
61_1_4.NIV: Through these he has given us his very great and precious promises, so that through them you may participate in the divine nature and escape the corruption in the world caused by evil desires.
61_1_4.NRSVCE: Thus he has given us, through these things, his precious and very great promises, so that through them you may escape from the corruption that is in the world because of lust, and may become participants of the divine nature.

61_1_9.NKJV: For he who lacks these things is shortsighted, even to blindness, and has forgotten that he was cleansed from his old sins.

Prophecy of Scripture

61_1_16.NKJV: For we did not follow cunningly devised fables when we made known to you the power and coming of our Lord Jesus Christ, but were eyewitnesses of His majesty.

61_1_16.ESV: For we did not follow cleverly devised myths when we made known to you the power and coming of our Lord Jesus Christ, but we were eyewitnesses of his majesty.

61_1_16.NLT: For we were not making up clever stories when we told you about the powerful coming of our Lord Jesus Christ. We saw his majestic splendor with our own eyes
61_1_16.WEB: For we did not follow cunningly devised fables, when we made known to you the power and coming of our Lord Jesus Christ, but we were eyewitnesses of his majesty.
61_1_16.CSB17: For we did not follow cleverly contrived myths when we made known to you the power and coming of our Lord Jesus Christ; <sup>ⓜ</sup> instead, we were eyewitnesses of his majesty. <sup>ⓝ</sup>
61_1_16.AMP: For we did not follow cleverly devised stories or myths when we made known to you the power and coming of our Lord Jesus Christ, but we were eyewitnesses of His majesty [His grandeur, His authority, His sovereignty].

61_1_19.NKJV: And so we have the prophetic word confirmed, which you do well to heed as a light that shines in a dark place, until the day dawns and the morning star rises in your hearts;
61_1_19.KJV: We have<S>2192</S> also<S>2532</S> a more sure<S>949</S> word<S>3056</S> of prophecy<S>4397</S>; whereunto<S>3739</S> ye do<S>4160</S> well<S>2573</S> that ye take heed<S>4337</S>, as<S>5613</S> unto a light<S>3088</S> that shineth<S>5316</S> in<S>1722</S> a dark<S>850</S> place<S>5117</S>, until<S>2193</S> <S>3739</S> the day<S>2250</S> dawn<S>1306</S>, and<S>2532</S> the day star<S>5459</S> arise<S>393</S> in<S>1722</S> your<S>5216</S> hearts<S>2588</S>:
61_1_19.ESV: And we have the prophetic word more fully confirmed, to which you will do well to pay attention as to a lamp shining in a dark place, until the day dawns and the morning star rises in your hearts,
61_1_19.NASB: [So] we have the prophetic word [made] more sure, to which you do well to pay attention as to a lamp shining in a dark place, until the day dawns and the morning star arises in your hearts.
61_1_19.NLT: Because of that experience, we have even greater confidence in the message proclaimed by the prophets. You must pay close attention to what they wrote, for their words are like a lamp shining in a dark place — until the Day dawns, and Christ the Morning Star shines in your hearts.

61_1_19.CSB17: We also have the prophetic word strongly confirmed, and you will do well to pay attention to it, as to a lamp shining in a dark place, <sup>ⓒ</sup> until the day dawns <sup>ⓓ</sup> and the morning star <sup>ⓔ</sup> rises in your hearts.
61_1_19.AMP: So we have the prophetic word made more certain. You do well to pay [close] attention to it as to a lamp shining in a dark place, until the day dawns and light breaks through the gloom and the morning star arises in your hearts.
61_1_19.MSG: We couldn't be more sure of what we saw and heard—God's glory, God's voice. The prophetic Word was confirmed to us. You'll do well to keep focusing on it. It's the one light you have in a dark time as you wait for daybreak and the rising of the Morning Star in your hearts.
61_1_19.NIV: And we have the word of the prophets made more certain, and you will do well to pay attention to it, as to a light shining in a dark place, until the day dawns and the morning star rises in your hearts.
61_1_19.NRSVCE: So we have the prophetic message more fully confirmed. You will do well to be attentive to this as to a lamp shining in a dark place, until the day dawns and the morning star rises in your hearts.
